title: Bergström Defends Vantara's Privacy Record Before Shareholders
body: Nils Bergström, the chief executive of Vantara AI, addressed shareholders Thursday in a video call following reports that the company faces regulatory action in Ireland. He called the coverage "inaccurate and premature" and said the company had received only a standard information request from the Data Protection Commission. "We have exchanged correspondence with the DPC before and always resolved matters cooperatively," Bergström said. He did not take questions from journalists on the call. Shares in Vantara, which listed on the Nasdaq Stockholm exchange in 2024, fell roughly four percent on Tuesday following the EuroPrivacy Wire report before recovering partially by the end of the week. Several analysts noted that regulatory uncertainty in Europe has become a standard risk factor for U.S.-facing AI companies, even those headquartered outside the United States. Bergström did not address reports of a potential partnership with a South Korean firm that have circulated in Korean business media over the past two weeks.
